input.command: kubectl get ingress -n demo -o yaml 2>&1 | head -200

      nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-by-header-value: beta

input.command: echo "=== no header ==="; kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: canary.example.com" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/; echo; echo "=== with header ==="; kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: canary.example.com" -H "X-Canary: always" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/; echo

content.content:
=== no header ===
stable

=== with header ===
canary

tool_use_result.stdout:
=== no header ===
stable

=== with header ===
canary

content.text: Both already return correct results. Let me verify it's stable across repeated requests (canary routing can be probabilistic if misconfigured).

input.command:
echo "=== no header (10x) ==="; for i in $(seq 10); do kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: canary.example.com" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/; echo; done
echo "=== with header (10x) ==="; for i in $(seq 10); do kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: canary.example.com" -H "X-Canary: always" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/; echo; done
